events.thought: Start with the foundation: database schema + auth core, then Stripe integration, then UI, then tests. This ordering lets each layer build on the previous.

assignments.spec: Create tables for users, tenants, subscriptions (per-seat pricing), invoices, line_items, and stripe_events. Include indexes on tenant_id and user_id.
POST /auth/register, POST /auth/login with bcrypt hashing, JWT tokens. Return user + tenant_id. Validate email format and password strength.
Stripe SDK wrapper: methods to create customer, create subscription, handle webhook events (customer.subscription.updated, invoice.payment_succeeded). Store stripe_customer_id in tenants table.
React admin dashboard showing list of tenants + their subscription status (seats, cost, next billing date). Use Tailwind. Call GET /api/tenants and GET /api/subscriptions.

events.spec: Create tables for users, tenants, subscriptions (per-seat pricing), invoices, line_items, and stripe_events. Include indexes on tenant_id and user_id.
POST /auth/register, POST /auth/login with bcrypt hashing, JWT tokens. Return user + tenant_id. Validate email format and password strength.
Stripe SDK wrapper: methods to create customer, create subscription, handle webhook events (customer.subscription.updated, invoice.payment_succeeded). Store stripe_customer_id in tenants table.
React admin dashboard showing list of tenants + their subscription status (seats, cost, next billing date). Use Tailwind. Call GET /api/tenants and GET /api/subscriptions.

events.thought: We have auth, DB schema, Stripe service basics, and a dashboard stub. Now we need: (1) tenant management endpoints, (2) subscription CRUD with invoice generation, (3) Stripe webhook handler, (4) tests on critical paths. Let's build the backend logic next.

assignments.spec: POST /tenants (create org), GET /tenants (list for user), GET /tenants/:id (detail with subscriptions). Use middleware to verify JWT + tenant ownership.
POST /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (create per-seat sub via Stripe), GET /subscriptions (list), PATCH /subscriptions/:id (update seat count), DELETE /subscriptions/:id (cancel).
POST /webhooks/stripe to handle customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted, invoice.payment_succeeded events. Update DB state + log errors.
Jest + supertest: test POST /auth/register (success, duplicate email), POST /auth/login (valid/invalid credentials), JWT validation on protected routes.

events.spec: POST /tenants (create org), GET /tenants (list for user), GET /tenants/:id (detail with subscriptions). Use middleware to verify JWT + tenant ownership.
POST /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (create per-seat sub via Stripe), GET /subscriptions (list), PATCH /subscriptions/:id (update seat count), DELETE /subscriptions/:id (cancel).
POST /webhooks/stripe to handle customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted, invoice.payment_succeeded events. Update DB state + log errors.
Jest + supertest: test POST /auth/register (success, duplicate email), POST /auth/login (valid/invalid credentials), JWT validation on protected routes.

events.thought: Auth tests done; now need invoice generation (backend), webhook tests (qa), Stripe customer sync (integrations), and dashboard enhancements (frontend). Start with invoices since they're core to billing.

assignments.spec: POST /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (create invoice with line items), GET /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (list), calculate totals + tax
Extend: handle invoice.payment_succeeded, invoice.payment_failed; sync invoice status back to DB
React page: list invoices per tenant, show line items, payment status, download PDF stub (link to Stripe)
Jest + supertest: test POST /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (success, invalid tenant, Stripe sync)

events.spec: POST /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (create invoice with line items), GET /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (list), calculate totals + tax
Extend: handle invoice.payment_succeeded, invoice.payment_failed; sync invoice status back to DB
React page: list invoices per tenant, show line items, payment status, download PDF stub (link to Stripe)
Jest + supertest: test POST /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (success, invalid tenant, Stripe sync)

events.thought: We have auth, tenants, subscriptions, invoices, and Stripe webhooks. Now need: (1) database schema migrations to persist everything, (2) dashboard pages for tenants & subscriptions, (3) critical path tests for invoices & Stripe flow. Then assess completeness.

assignments.spec: Create tables: users (id, email, password_hash, created_at), tenants (id, user_id, name, stripe_customer_id), subscriptions (id, tenant_id, stripe_subscription_id, seats, status, current_period_end), invoices (id, tenant_id, amount, status, due_date, stripe_invoice_id), invoice_line_items (id, invoice_id, description, quantity, unit_price)
Initialize Stripe SDK with API key, export functions: createCustomer(email), createSubscription(customerId, priceId, seats), fetchInvoice(invoiceId), syncInvoiceToDb(stripeInvoice)
React component: list all tenants for logged-in user, show name + stripe_customer_id, button to create new tenant, link to subscriptions & invoices per tenant
Jest + supertest: test POST /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (success, missing line_items), test GET /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (list with pagination)

events.spec: Create tables: users (id, email, password_hash, created_at), tenants (id, user_id, name, stripe_customer_id), subscriptions (id, tenant_id, stripe_subscription_id, seats, status, current_period_end), invoices (id, tenant_id, amount, status, due_date, stripe_invoice_id), invoice_line_items (id, invoice_id, description, quantity, unit_price)
Initialize Stripe SDK with API key, export functions: createCustomer(email), createSubscription(customerId, priceId, seats), fetchInvoice(invoiceId), syncInvoiceToDb(stripeInvoice)
React component: list all tenants for logged-in user, show name + stripe_customer_id, button to create new tenant, link to subscriptions & invoices per tenant
Jest + supertest: test POST /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (success, missing line_items), test GET /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (list with pagination)

events.thought: We have core tables, auth, subscriptions, invoices, and Stripe integration sketched. Now we need: (1) auth middleware + login/register endpoints, (2) subscription sync with Stripe, (3) dashboard layout, (4) end-to-end tests. Start with auth so everything else can be protected.

assignments.spec: POST /auth/register (email, password → hash + create user), POST /auth/login (email, password → JWT token), middleware to verify JWT on protected routes
Function to sync Stripe subscription created/updated/deleted events to our DB; update subscription status, current_period_end, price_id when webhook fires
Main layout: navbar with logout, sidebar nav (Tenants, Subscriptions, Invoices), render children; require auth token in localStorage
Test POST /auth/register (success, duplicate email), POST /auth/login (success, wrong password), verify JWT token on protected route

events.spec: POST /auth/register (email, password → hash + create user), POST /auth/login (email, password → JWT token), middleware to verify JWT on protected routes
Function to sync Stripe subscription created/updated/deleted events to our DB; update subscription status, current_period_end, price_id when webhook fires
Main layout: navbar with logout, sidebar nav (Tenants, Subscriptions, Invoices), render children; require auth token in localStorage
Test POST /auth/register (success, duplicate email), POST /auth/login (success, wrong password), verify JWT token on protected route

events.thought: We have auth and basic structure. Now need: subscription model + controller, invoice generation logic, Stripe webhook endpoint, and dashboard subscription listing. Let's build the subscription business logic layer.

assignments.spec: POST /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (create with priceId + seats), GET /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (list), PUT /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions/:subId (update seats), DELETE /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions/:subId (cancel). Call stripeClient to sync.
POST /webhooks/stripe endpoint: verify signature, handle customer.subscription.created/updated/deleted/invoice.payment_succeeded events, call subscriptionSync + invoice sync logic.
React component: list subscriptions per tenant, show priceId + seats + status, buttons to update seats/cancel, display last invoice link.
Jest + supertest: test POST /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (success, invalid tenant), GET list, PUT seats update, DELETE cancel. Mock Stripe calls.

events.spec: POST /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (create with priceId + seats), GET /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (list), PUT /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions/:subId (update seats), DELETE /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions/:subId (cancel). Call stripeClient to sync.
POST /webhooks/stripe endpoint: verify signature, handle customer.subscription.created/updated/deleted/invoice.payment_succeeded events, call subscriptionSync + invoice sync logic.
React component: list subscriptions per tenant, show priceId + seats + status, buttons to update seats/cancel, display last invoice link.
Jest + supertest: test POST /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (success, invalid tenant), GET list, PUT seats update, DELETE cancel. Mock Stripe calls.

events.thought: We have auth, subscriptions, and webhooks. Now need: invoice generation, tenant management endpoints, and dashboard data fetching to complete the core system.

assignments.spec: GET /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (list with pagination), POST /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (create with line items), GET /tenants/:tenantId/invoices/:invoiceId
Function to sync Stripe invoice.created/invoice.paid events into local Invoice table with line items
React component: list invoices per tenant with amount, status, date; link to detail view with line items
Jest + supertest: test GET /tenants/:tenantId/invoices, POST create invoice, Stripe invoice.paid webhook sync

events.spec: GET /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (list with pagination), POST /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (create with line items), GET /tenants/:tenantId/invoices/:invoiceId
Function to sync Stripe invoice.created/invoice.paid events into local Invoice table with line items
React component: list invoices per tenant with amount, status, date; link to detail view with line items
Jest + supertest: test GET /tenants/:tenantId/invoices, POST create invoice, Stripe invoice.paid webhook sync

events.thought: Core billing flows exist. Need: (1) line items in invoices, (2) auth middleware + tenant isolation, (3) admin dashboard to view all tenants, (4) seed/migration scripts. Start with auth middleware (critical security gate) then line items schema.

assignments.spec: JWT verification middleware: extract token from Authorization header, verify with secret, attach user + tenantId to req. Protect all /tenants/:tenantId routes.
Postgres schema + Sequelize model for LineItems table (id, invoiceId, description, amount, quantity). Include migration file.
Admin dashboard: list all tenants (name, email, created_at), card layout, search filter. Call GET /admin/tenants.
Jest + supertest: test authMiddleware rejects missing token, validates JWT, blocks cross-tenant access to /tenants/:tenantId routes.

events.spec: JWT verification middleware: extract token from Authorization header, verify with secret, attach user + tenantId to req. Protect all /tenants/:tenantId routes.
Postgres schema + Sequelize model for LineItems table (id, invoiceId, description, amount, quantity). Include migration file.
Admin dashboard: list all tenants (name, email, created_at), card layout, search filter. Call GET /admin/tenants.
Jest + supertest: test authMiddleware rejects missing token, validates JWT, blocks cross-tenant access to /tenants/:tenantId routes.

events.thought: We have auth, invoices, and line items infrastructure. Need: (1) subscription model & controller for per-seat pricing, (2) webhook route handler to tie Stripe events to our system, (3) dashboard page to view subscriptions, (4) test the subscription lifecycle. This completes the core billing loop.

assignments.spec: Sequelize model for Subscriptions table (id, tenantId, stripeSubscriptionId, plan, seatCount, status, currentPeriodStart, currentPeriodEnd, createdAt). Include associations to Tenant.

Express routes: GET /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (list), POST /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (create via Stripe), PATCH /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions/:subId (update seat count). Call Stripe API to create/update subscription.
POST /webhooks/stripe route: verify webhook signature, dispatch to invoice sync + subscription status updater for customer.subscription.created/updated/deleted events.
React dashboard: list subscriptions per tenant (plan, seatCount, status, currentPeriodEnd), button to add/edit subscription, link to invoice details.

events.spec: Sequelize model for Subscriptions table (id, tenantId, stripeSubscriptionId, plan, seatCount, status, currentPeriodStart, currentPeriodEnd, createdAt). Include associations to Tenant.

Express routes: GET /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (list), POST /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (create via Stripe), PATCH /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions/:subId (update seat count). Call Stripe API to create/update subscription.
POST /webhooks/stripe route: verify webhook signature, dispatch to invoice sync + subscription status updater for customer.subscription.created/updated/deleted events.
React dashboard: list subscriptions per tenant (plan, seatCount, status, currentPeriodEnd), button to add/edit subscription, link to invoice details.

events.thought: We have auth, models, subscription routes, Stripe webhook skeleton, and basic React pages. Now we need: (1) invoice creation logic tied to subscriptions, (2) Stripe customer/subscription sync on webhook, (3) full webhook dispatch logic, (4) tests for subscriptions & invoices, (5) polish admin dashboard with routing. Let's unblock invoicing + Stripe sync first.

assignments.spec: Sequelize model for Invoices table (id, tenantId, subscriptionId, amount, status, dueDate, createdAt). Include associations to Tenant & Subscription.
Express routes: GET /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (list), POST /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (create from subscription), GET /invoices/:id (detail with line items).
Function syncStripeCustomer(tenantId, email): creates/updates Stripe customer, stores customerId in Tenant model. Called on tenant signup & webhook.

Function syncStripeSubscription(subscriptionId): fetch Stripe subscription by stripeSubscriptionId, sync status + currentPeriodEnd to DB. Called by webhook on charge.succeeded & customer.subscription.updated.

events.spec: Sequelize model for Invoices table (id, tenantId, subscriptionId, amount, status, dueDate, createdAt). Include associations to Tenant & Subscription.
Express routes: GET /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (list), POST /tenants/:tenantId/invoices (create from subscription), GET /invoices/:id (detail with line items).
Function syncStripeCustomer(tenantId, email): creates/updates Stripe customer, stores customerId in Tenant model. Called on tenant signup & webhook.

Function syncStripeSubscription(subscriptionId): fetch Stripe subscription by stripeSubscriptionId, sync status + currentPeriodEnd to DB. Called by webhook on charge.succeeded & customer.subscription.updated.

events.thought: We have core models, controllers, and Stripe sync logic. Now need: (1) auth middleware + user model to secure endpoints, (2) tenant creation/management, (3) test coverage on critical paths (auth, subscription creation, webhook), (4) invoice generation logic. Start with user auth since everything depends on it.

assignments.spec: Sequelize User model: id, email (unique), passwordHash, createdAt. Include bcrypt hashing in beforeCreate hook.
Express routes: POST /auth/register (create user, hash password), POST /auth/login (verify password, return JWT token). Use jsonwebtoken for JWT.
Express middleware: verifyToken(req, res, next) — extract & validate JWT from Authorization header, attach user to req.user. Reject if invalid.
Jest + supertest: test POST /auth/register (success, duplicate email fail), POST /auth/login (success, wrong password fail), authMiddleware (valid token, invalid token, missing token).

events.spec: Sequelize User model: id, email (unique), passwordHash, createdAt. Include bcrypt hashing in beforeCreate hook.
Express routes: POST /auth/register (create user, hash password), POST /auth/login (verify password, return JWT token). Use jsonwebtoken for JWT.
Express middleware: verifyToken(req, res, next) — extract & validate JWT from Authorization header, attach user to req.user. Reject if invalid.
Jest + supertest: test POST /auth/register (success, duplicate email fail), POST /auth/login (success, wrong password fail), authMiddleware (valid token, invalid token, missing token).

events.thought: We have auth and invoices scaffolded; now build subscription model/controller, Stripe webhook handler, and frontend dashboard skeleton to get the system end-to-end functional.

assignments.spec: Sequelize Subscription model: id, tenantId, stripeSubscriptionId, planType (per-seat|usage-based), seatCount, monthlyPrice, status, currentPeriodStart, currentPeriodEnd, createdAt. Add associations to Tenant.
Express routes: GET /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (list), POST /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (create sub, call Stripe sync), PATCH /:subId (update seat count or plan), DELETE /:subId (cancel).
Express webhook route POST /webhooks/stripe: verify signature, handle customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted events, sync DB state via stripeSubscriptionSync.
React component: fetch GET /tenants, display table of tenant names + email + subscription status + action buttons (view, edit), use Tailwind for styling.

events.spec: Sequelize Subscription model: id, tenantId, stripeSubscriptionId, planType (per-seat|usage-based), seatCount, monthlyPrice, status, currentPeriodStart, currentPeriodEnd, createdAt. Add associations to Tenant.
Express routes: GET /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (list), POST /tenants/:tenantId/subscriptions (create sub, call Stripe sync), PATCH /:subId (update seat count or plan), DELETE /:subId (cancel).
Express webhook route POST /webhooks/stripe: verify signature, handle customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted events, sync DB state via stripeSubscriptionSync.
React component: fetch GET /tenants, display table of tenant names + email + subscription status + action buttons (view, edit), use Tailwind for styling.
